summary: Proof of gate-evidence readiness is not sufficiently current, and oversized-PR-body fallback-path coverage is incomplete for the force-bound branch.
inspection_summary: Inspected review request constraints, verification evidence, PR prepare artifact, source PR body-limit implementation in src/pr-manager.js, and the related regression tests in test/vibepro-cli.test.js; checked HEAD binding and command records for freshness, gate readiness, and path coverage.
judgment_delta: Initial assumption of PASS from passing unit/typecheck commands -> downgraded to needs_changes because verification inputs are marked stale and one body-limit fallback strategy is not covered by tests.
Initial assumption of PASS from test coverage -> downgraded to needs_changes because only artifact_reference_fallback is validated for oversized body; forced fallback path is untested.

findings.detail: Evidence inputs are currently stale yet treated as the basis for this gate context: both verification-evidence.json and pr-prepare.json report evidence_reuse.status stale, fresh_use_allowed false, and gate_status needs_refresh because risk_surface_fingerprint changed; this weakens command/evidence freshness binding for a gate_evidence review.

Regression/path-surface coverage misses the forced fallback branch of PR body limit handling: resolvePrBodyForGithub can return strategy forced_artifact_reference_fallback via forceBoundPrBody(...) in src/pr-manager.js, but test/vibepro-cli.test.js only asserts successful use of artifact_reference_fallback for oversized body and does not prove the forced branch or its user-facing warning/evidence trail is covered.
